person.name: Sir Richard Maisterson II of Ferns

nameForms.fullText: Sir Richard Maisterson II of Ferns

sibling.name: Sir Richard Maisterson II of Ferns

km.Nantwich, Cheshire, England: ណានតវិ្, ឆេសហីរេ, ប្រទេសអង់គ្លេស

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Richard Maisterson II of Ferns於大約1523年出生在英國爾南特維其時，他的父親Thomas Maisterson of Nantwich29歲，母親Margery Mainwaring25歲。他和Joan Beeching至少有1個兒子和1個女兒。他去世在愛爾蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Richard Maisterson II of Ferns于大约1523年出生在英国爾南特維其时，他的父亲Thomas Maisterson of Nantwich29岁，母亲Margery Mainwaring25岁。他和Joan Beeching至少有1个儿子和1个女儿。他去世在爱尔兰。

nameMeanings.name: Maisterson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English maister ‘master’ + -son. Compare Master .
Irish: in Ireland, this is usually the English name in 1 above. However, it was also adopted for Irish Mac an Mháighistir ‘son of the master’, also Anglicized as McMaster .

variants.name: Master
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Amar, Dhimant, Pravin, Amit, Aruna, Bhanu, Gaurang, Gopal, Haren, Jagu, Jayesh, Kalpesh.

English: occupational name from Middle English maister ‘master’ (Old French maistre). The word had a wide range of applications, and the reference in surnames may be to a schoolmaster, to the master of a farm or house, to the master of an apprentice, or to a ship's captain. In Scotland the eldest sons of barons and the uncles of lords were called Masters, but this is not primarily a Scottish surname.
German: dialect form of   Meister   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

variants.name: McMaster
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac an Mháighistir ‘son of the master’, meaning ‘teacher’ or ‘cleric’. It could also be applied in the sense of ‘lord’ or ‘owner’. This name was sometimes Anglicized as Masterson .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.description: Henry VIII dies, succeeded by the boy king Edward VI. England and Ireland were ruled by the senior nobility of England.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.
Accession of James I. Surrender of Hugh O'Neill. Enforcement of English Law in Ireland.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

Great Catholic-Gaelic rebellion for return of lands, later joined by Old English Catholics in Ireland. Under leadership of Irish chieftain, Rory O'More, conspiracy was formed to seize Dublin and expel the English. English settlers were driven out of Ulster. Catholics hold 59% of land in Ireland.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Richard Maisterson II of Ferns was born about 1523, in Nantwich, Cheshire, England, his father, Thomas Maisterson of Nantwich, was 29 and his mother, Margery Mainwaring, was 25. He had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with Joan Beeching. He died in Ireland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Richard Maisterson II of Ferns est né environ 1523, à Nantwich, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Maisterson of Nantwich, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Margery Mainwaring, avait 25 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille avec Joan Beeching. Il est décédé à Irlande.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Richard Maisterson II of Ferns um 1523 in Nantwich, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas Maisterson of Nantwich 29 und seine Mutter Margery Mainwaring 25. Er hatte mit Joan Beeching mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb in Irland.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard Maisterson II of Ferns è nato il circa 1523 nel Nantwich, Cheshire, England, il suo padre, Thomas Maisterson of Nantwich, aveva 29 anni e il suo madre, Margery Mainwaring, aveva 25 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia con Joan Beeching. Morì nel Irlanda.

ja.Nantwich, Cheshire, England: イギリスチェシュアイアナントウィチ
ja.Ashford, Kent, England: イギリスケントアシュフォード
ja.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Maisterson II of Fernsが約1523年にイギリスチェシュアイアナントウィチで生まれた時，父Thomas Maisterson of Nantwichは29歳，母Margery Mainwaringは25歳であった。彼はJoan Beechingとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼はアイルランドで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Maisterson II of Ferns(이)가 대략 1523년 영국 체실르 난트위치에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Maisterson of Nantwich(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Margery Mainwaring(은)는 25세였다. 그는 Joan Beeching(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 아일랜드에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard Maisterson II of Ferns nasceu aproximadamente 1523, em Nantwich, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Maisterson of Nantwich, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Margery Mainwaring, tinha 25 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha com Joan Beeching. Ele faleceu em Irlanda.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Richard Maisterson II of Ferns родился около 1523 г. в местности нантвич, чешир, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas Maisterson of Nantwich было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Margery Mainwaring, 25. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь от Joan Beeching. Он умер в местности Ирландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Richard Maisterson II of Ferns nació aproximadamente 1523, en Nantwich, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas Maisterson of Nantwich, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Margery Mainwaring, tenía 25 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija con Joan Beeching. Murió en Irlanda.
